principle: Choose typefaces that are either clearly the same (concordant) or clearly different (contrasting) — similar-but-different typefaces create conflict that looks like a mistake rather than a design decision
explanation: X.ui|type_combination_rule: three_states: (1)concordant: one_typeface+its_variations -> calm+formal (2)conflict: two_typefaces_with_similar_characteristics -> looks_like_mistake (3)contrast: two_typefaces_very_different_in_structure+weight+form -> intentional+exciting; diagnosis: wrong_combination -> look_for_SIMILARITIES_not_differences; similarities_cause_conflict; solution: find_similarities -> eliminate_or_maximize_difference; NO:two_serif_faces_that_look_similar NO:two_scripts_together; eg: Opal_Regular+ITC_Baskerville_together >> feels_like_mistake >> Opal+strong_sans_serif >> clear_contrast

principle: Contrast typefaces across six dimensions — size, weight, structure, form, direction, and color — and combine multiple contrasts for maximum effect rather than relying on a single difference
explanation: X.ui|six_type_contrasts: size: big_vs_small; weight: heavy_vs_light(NOT_medium_vs_medium); structure: monoweight_sans_vs_thick-thin_serif; form: caps_vs_lowercase+roman_vs_italic; direction: wide_horizontal_vs_tall_narrow_columns; color: black_vs_gray+warm_vs_cool; rule: rarely_one_contrast_is_effective -> combine_multiple; NO:contrasting_only_medium_weight_with_slightly_heavier NO:one_tiny_difference_as_sole_contrast; eg: Helvetica_headline+Helvetica_body >> Aachen_Bold_large+Arno_small_serif_body

principle: Never combine two script typefaces or a script with an italic — both share flowing slanted form creating conflict; pair scripts with strong roman faces instead
explanation: X.ui|no_script_italic_combo: scripts+italics: share_similar_form(cursive+flowing+slanted); two_scripts_together: too_many_similarities -> conflict; script+italic: same_form_type -> conflict; fix: combine_script_with_roman_face; maximum_contrast: script(flowing)+monoweight_sans_OR_slab_serif -> clearly_different_in_every_way; NO:two_calligraphic_scripts_on_same_piece NO:script_with_italic_body_copy; eg: Peoni_script+Goudy_Oldstyle_Italic >> conflict >> Peoni_script+Aachen_Bold_roman >> clear_contrast

principle: Set extended body copy in oldstyle serif typefaces — their moderate thick/thin transitions are invisible at reading size and let readers absorb content without the type calling attention to itself
explanation: X.ui|oldstyle_for_body: oldstyle_features: diagonal_stress+bracketed_serifs+moderate_thick-thin -> based_on_pen_handlettering; readability: no_distinguishing_characteristics -> doesnt_interrupt_reading -> best_for_extensive_text; avoid_for_body: modern_typefaces(Bodoni+Didot): radical_thick-thin+vertical_stress -> dazzling_at_small_sizes+thin_lines_disappear; modern_typefaces_for: display_only+large_headlines; oldstyle_examples: Garamond+Palatino+Times+Sabon+Goudy; NO:Bodoni_for_body_copy NO:modern_typeface_at_text_sizes; eg: Bodoni_body_copy -> dazzling+hard_to_read >> Garamond_body -> invisible+readable

principle: Invest in a typeface family with multiple weights rather than multiple families — light through ultra-black within one family enables dramatic contrast while guaranteeing harmony
explanation: X.ui|type_family_contrast: combining_different_typefaces: risk_of_conflict+requires_skill; single_family_multiple_weights: Avenir_Next_Condensed_UltraLight+Heavy+Italic -> dramatic_contrast+guaranteed_harmony; benefit: all_members_share_underlying_structure -> no_conflict_possible; sans_serif_families: often_have_light_to_ultra_black+compressed -> maximum_range; investment: one_good_family_5+_weights -> better_than_5_separate_typefaces; NO:only_owning_Helvetica_and_Arial NO:contrasting_medium_with_regular; eg: Helvetica_Regular+Helvetica_Bold_weak >> Avenir_Condensed_UltraLight+Avenir_Heavy_dramatic
